arguments.command:
for pkg in System.Text.Json Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting Microsoft.Extensions.Logging Azure.Storage.Blobs Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting xunit; do
  ver=$(curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/${pkg,,}/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; versions=[v for v in json.load(sys.stdin)['versions'] if '-' not in v]; print(versions[-1])" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$pkg -> $ver"
done

Now I need to check NuGet for the latest available versions of these packages to see what updates are available.

No hardcoded versions remain — all packages now use MSBuild properties from `Directory.Build.props`.

**Summary of changes:**

- **`Directory.Build.props`** — single source of truth for all 8 version properties; bumped `BlobsVersion` (12.24.0→12.27.0), `HostingVersion` (8.0.1→10.0.7), added `SystemTextJsonVersion` (10.0.7), `LoggingVersion` (10.0.7), `NewtonsoftJsonNet8Version` (8.0.26), `OpenTelemetryHostingVersion` (1.15.3), `XunitVersion` (2.9.3), `NewtonsoftJsonNet6Version` (6.0.36)
- **`Common.props`** — version now uses `$(LoggingVersion)` property
- **All 5 csproj files** — replaced every hardcoded version with the corresponding `$(…)` property, eliminating version drift (e.g., `System.Text.Json` was 8.0.5/9.0.0/10.0.1 across projects, now uniformly 10.0.7)
